per_bead.note: SelectWork (internal/next/select.go) is a pure unit-testable func; existing TestSelectWork_* prove the harness. Claim path (next.go L213) calls SelectWork(items,pick) and IGNORES the positional `args` (args reaches only runEmitOnly, L58/L72) => a name-aware-selection RED test fails on current code, passes after fix (genuine). help_golden harness real: buildMindspecBinary (testhelpers_test.go L21) + exec subprocess + grep (help_golden_test.go). Current `next` long help mentions the positional ONLY under --emit-only (next.go L36-37), so a help-golden asserting the claim-path wording is genuinely RED. positional-vs-pick conflict guard is also new behavior.

newRepoExecutor(t) confirmed (executor_test.go L112): real temp-git repo + recording fakeWorktreeOps (removeCalls/onRemove L27/L51-56) + MindspecExecutor. CheckDirtyTree exists (next/guard.go L110). TestWithWorkingDir_RemovedCwdRemainsAtDirSilently exists (L994) and does a REAL os.RemoveAll + os.Getwd recovery assertion — the cwd-safety AC is structurally writable ONLY in the executor package (where removal really deletes), exactly where the plan places it. release.go does not yet exist => all TestRelease* are genuinely new+RED. runBDCombFn/runBDFn inject seams real (beads.go L26-30). CAVEAT (non-blocking): the unexported `fakeWorktreeOps` lives in package executor and is NOT importable from cmd/mindspec(package main); the cmd-level ordering/dirty TestRelease* must either use the exported MockExecutor (mock.go) or define a 3-method local fake of the exported WorktreeOps interface — still hermetic, but the plan's 'reuse internal/executor injected-fake' phrasing slightly overstates direct reuse.

ClaimBead (beads.go L162-168) RED via runBDCombFn inject — pattern proven by existing TestClaimBead_PropagatesError (next_test.go L408). Doctor: findCheck (beads_test.go L12), beadsRoot (L34), stubBd (L340), t.Setenv(PATH), checkBdVersionFloor (beads.go L284) all exist; the two new checks are additive. 'two bd on PATH' check is pure filesystem/PATH => fully hermetic, no stub-output dependency. CAVEAT (non-blocking, must be honored at impl): current ClaimBead error ALREADY embeds string(out) (L165), so an assertion that merely 'contains depends_on_id' is VACUOUS — the load-bearing RED assertion is that the error is NOT prefixed with the misleading 'may already be claimed' string. The plan states both halves; the not-flattened half is the genuine pin and must be the failing one. stubBd echoes one static output regardless of bd subcommand — fine for the single-command schema-drift sim.

gitutil seam confirmed: var execCommand = exec.Command (gitops.go L22), swapExec (gitops_test.go L276) captures every invocation's name+args and stubs stdout+exitCode via /bin/sh, assertArgs (L321) asserts argv; HasRemote (L225) + rejectOptionLike conventions exist. InitSpecWorkspace (executor) currently does unconditional CreateBranch(specBranch,'HEAD') => detect/fetch/branch-from-origin is new => RED genuine. CAVEAT (non-blocking): swapExec applies ONE stdout/exitCode to ALL captured commands, so the 'empty/garbage symbolic-ref FALLS THROUGH to git remote show origin' sub-case needs a per-command-aware stub (a small extension of swapExec) since symbolic-ref and remote-show want different outputs; the primary detect (symbolic-ref -> origin/develop) and the non-zero-fetch WARN-fallback cases are writable as-is.

summary: APPROVE. All four beads' RED tests are writable hermetically (go build + filtered go test, no internal/harness) with machinery that I confirmed by reading the cited files: SelectWork unit + buildMindspecBinary help-golden (B1); newRepoExecutor real temp-git + fakeWorktreeOps.onRemove + CheckDirtyTree + TestWithWorkingDir_RemovedCwdRemainsAtDirSilently cwd pattern (B2); runBDCombFn inject + findCheck/beadsRoot/stubBd/Setenv(PATH) (B3); execCommand/swapExec/assertArgs argv-capture seam (B4). Each names new surface that fails on current code (claim path ignores positional; release.go absent; ClaimBead misleading prefix; InitSpecWorkspace hardcoded HEAD). Three non-blocking impl caveats recorded: (B2) the unexported fakeWorktreeOps is not importable from package main — cmd-level release test needs MockExecutor or a tiny local WorktreeOps fake; (B3) the contains-'depends_on_id' assertion alone is vacuous — the not-'may already be claimed' assertion is the load-bearing pin; (B4) swapExec stubs all commands uniformly, so the garbage-symbolic-ref fall-through needs a per-command stub variant. None defeats hermeticity or makes a test unwritable, so no CHANGES.
